You have every opportunity to not see the things you don't want to see. If you do want to see more of this nonsense follow #transmemes on imgur.
item.dc:creator: GwensBackOnHerBS

item.title: I’m hoping that maybe this will lead to a way to develop a stronger anti-nausea medicine without the horrifying side effects
item.description:
This isn’t going to be as in detail as my usual posts. I just haven’t been feeling well lately, but I did want to share this really exciting thing that I learned! I want to emphasize that this is not a brand new article - it’s just brand new information for me. I got curious about the thalidomide tragedy, and decided to do a little research. I really wanted to know if scientists had figured out WHY thalidomide was so destructive to fetal development when it originally possibly saved a few peoples’ lives, and I found this interesting article. It’s old, but it still gives me hope for the future! . The thalidomide tragedy is a lesser known event in today’s time, so I’ll do a quick recap. The TLDR: Thalidomide was an active ingredient in medication to treat pregnancy-related nausea. It later turned out that thalidomide caused disastrous birth deformities for reasons that scientists didn’t understand until 2018. The reasons are in the screen capture. . Now for the longer version, aka, the rest of this post…
As we all know, pregnancy comes with the infamous “morning sickness” phase, where a pregnant person experiences bouts of nausea and vomiting - especially if triggered by smells. There is an even more dangerous pregnancy complication called hyperemesis gravidarum (HG), where the nausea is not only intensified, but it lasts almost the entire pregnancy. HG is life-threatening. A pregnant person can’t hold down food or fluids. It has actually killed people before, including Charlotte Brontë, the famous author of Jane Eyre. If you’d like to learn more, you can read my post on it here: https://imgur.com/gallery/hyperemesis-gravidarum-is-pregnancy-related-fight-life-i-wanted-to-help-make-that-fight-least-tiny-bit-easier-AUfd38K
I didn’t find much on how the chemical actually came to be discovered and used medicinally. However, I’ve also been really unwell, so it’s possible that I just missed this part when doing my research. Regardless, the point is that people were suffering and on the verge of death from HG, and in 1958, scientists started using thalidomide to treat them. 10,000 children were born with a thalidomide complication. They had either severely deformed limbs or no limbs at all. There were many stillbirths as well. The historical drama “Call The Midwife” did several episodes on the tragedy, and one involved a baby that was born so deformed that they couldn’t even determine the sex. The baby died very soon after birth.
Because my focus is on a scientific article I read, I wanted to keep this part short. However, it should still be acknowledged that not nearly as many babies would’ve died had life actually taken precedence over profit. I can shorten this down to a parody I wrote years ago: ♪ ♪ Tale as old as time, song as old as rhyme, CEOs are greeedddyyy ♪ ♪
I’m questioning myself on whether I went a little too far with this meme. I hope it’s not too offensive. I feel like if I’d been one of the scientists in this situation, this would’ve been my response to it. Pure confusion, bewilderment, and frustration. It was saving pregnant patients but killing their babies. For those that were truly caring about their patients, this must’ve been horrifying. I can only imagine the guilt they felt. For decades, scientists puzzled over the mystery. In 2018, they made this breakthrough. I’m going to do the best of my ability to explain what I understand from the article, but it’s not going to explain everything because I’m not a scientist that understands all of the details (or even most of them).
This is the section that summarizes the study. These are also a lot of words, so let’s break this down to the part about what thalidomide does in the body.
That’s much better, but it’s still a lot of words. We can break this down further.
When I first made this image, I tried really hard to include examples. It made the image so small you had to squint to read it, so I’ll settle for the captions instead. . PROMOTING Promoting in this case can be done by either triggering a response, or making a response more intense. For example, oxygen promotes fire. The oxygen itself isn’t flammable, but exposing oxygen to a preexisting flame will make the fire bigger. Gasoline also promotes fire. If you throw a match onto a puddle of gasoline, the combination will trigger a fire. Depending on how much gas this puddle has, it could even cause an explosion. In the case of thalidomide, it was acting as a match to gasoline in the sense that it triggered this malfunction. . DEGRADATION Degradation is the noun for “degrading,” which in chemistry means the breaking down of a substance. An example of degradation in chemistry: Alzheimer’s disease is caused by a degradation/deterioration of brain cells, meaning they break down until the brain can no longer function. With thalidomide, the SALL4 was breaking down. . TRANSCRIPTION FACTORS Transcription Factors are like the article said, a type of cell protein that switches genes on or off. Transcription factors go beyond my comprehension. My limited understanding is that while there are multiple factors to turning a gene on/off, transcription factors are an essential protein in making that happen. To use an analogy, there are many steps to baking a cake, but unless you have an oven, everything else is pointless. I believe that transcription factors are sort of like an oven in that regard.
Image Source: https://www.researchgate.net/figure/Sall4-gene-expression-in-murine-models-pink-cells-represent-Sall4-expression-foremost_fig1_371401333 . This is a picture of how SALL4 develops in a mouse fetus because it’s the simplest picture that I could find. SALL4 is the gene that affects the growing of limbs and certain organs in the fetus. Thalidomide destroys SALL4, making the fetus unable to produce fully functioning limbs, or in some cases, any limbs at all.
It’s a long shot, but I have at least a sliver of hope that with this discovery, perhaps scientists can find a way to make a medication that keeps the nausea-relieving parts of thalidomide, while removing or neutralizing the parts that destroy SALL4. In the beginning, thalidomide did a lot of good for pregnant patients with HG that were on the verge of death. I really hope that we can find a way to make that a possible treatment again.
The 2018 Article: https://www.dana-farber.org/newsroom/news-releases/2018/after-60-years-scientists-uncover-how-thalidomide-produced-birth-defects The History of The Thalidomide Tragedy: https://judithjohnsonphd.com/2019/04/12/thalidomide-nine-things-you-didnt-know-about-the-poisonous-pill/ Another Article on The History of the Thalidomide Tragedy: https://www.thalidomide-tragedy.com/the-history-of-the-thalidomide-tragedy About Hyperemesis Gravidarum (HG): https://undefiningmotherhood.com/hyperemesis/ About Transcription Factors: https://www.khanacademy.org/science/ap-biology/gene-expression-and-regulation/regulation-of-gene-expression-and-cell-specialization/a/eukaryotic-transcription-factors For those that are scientifically educated (which doesn’t include me), here’s an interesting article about SALL4 development in mice: https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0267273 This is a REALLY INTERESTING article about thalidomide and veterinarian testing: https://www.understandinganimalresearch.org.uk/news/sixty-years-on-the-history-of-the-thalidomide-tragedy
item.dc:creator: OlivetheNerd

item.title: GovBrief Today #466
item.description: The Cultural Landscape Foundation sued today to stop the $13.1 million paint job on the floor of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool. The original quote was $1.5 million. The Trump administration skipped competitive bidding, the normal process for federal contracts this size. They handed the work to a Virginia company that painted the swimming pool at Trump's golf club. Trump wanted turquoise, "like in the Bahamas." The owner talked him down to "American flag blue." Trump's motorcade drove him across the drained pool last Thursday so he could inspect the paint. For over a century, architect Henry Bacon's Lincoln Memorial and Reflecting Pool have mirrored the grandeur of the National Mall. Trump decided it should instead look like a three-star hotel in Nassau, with a job that will pay one of his vendors more than eight times what he told the American people. Use 5 Calls or Resistbot to tell your representatives to open an investigation into Trump's latest grift. I've emailed 7,353 stories over 466 straight nights, free and without ads. The algorithm can't show them all to you. Add your name at govbrief.today and I will. #GovBriefToday #Resist Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com Don't miss pictures, data, and easy-click links on the website: https://go.govbrief.today/260511-full [1] https://go.govbrief.today/assassination-theories [2] https://go.govbrief.today/hamilton-fema [3] https://go.govbrief.today/lake-mastriano [4] https://go.govbrief.today/iran-war-may11 [5] https://go.govbrief.today/mo-court-map [6] https://go.govbrief.today/alabama-map-scotus [7] https://go.govbrief.today/va-map-scotus [8] https://go.govbrief.today/warsh-advances [9] https://go.govbrief.today/mife-extended [10] https://go.govbrief.today/appeals-mandatory-detention [11] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-dozes-again [12] https://go.govbrief.today/cummins-nomination [13] https://go.govbrief.today/wh-shithouse [14] https://go.govbrief.today/reflecting-pool-overrun [15] https://go.govbrief.today/trump-mobile [16] https://go.govbrief.today/reflect-pool-suit Subscribe (It's Free!) - https://gbounacos.substack.com IMGUR REMINDER: Got a lovely private message from a relatively new reader so THANKS! Imgur took away my account's ability to respond to private messages so it's frustrating to see them, but not be able to respond. Thank you again!
item.dc:creator: GeorgeBounacos

item.dc:creator: hoopsnek

item.title: Roll for initiative! D&D memes! Why? Because one kid graduates college this month and the next day the other is getting married. Huzzah! Best treasure ever.
item.description:
Actually it’s called anxiety and it’s an inherent trait.
I was there, Gandalf. 3,000 years ago. I saw it for myself.
I tried to mix up the wordy memes with the pretty pictures just like D&D does between math rocks and fighting stuff.
It’s the Human Distribution System.
I am the night.
In fact, my kiddos (son and soon to be Charlie-in-law) got artisanal wedding rings with red stones in them.
There’s this show called Upload which I really enjoy. One of the funny parts is all the NPCs are played by this one ginger actor playing all of them and because they’re not main characters, the designers didn’t invest resources to make them intelligent. It’s the perfect template.
Can confirm. Also use the turning lane as a passing lane during red lights.
“I run one of the largest gnome spotting websites in the country and we just got a huge spike in gnome movement. Over 25,000 gnomes on the move but we are months away from their traditional migration season. something BIG is going on.”
I play a gnomish enchanter named Gnerf whose backstory is he’s the black sheep of a gnomish cheese empire who wanted to go into magic to woo women rather than stay in the family business. He’s uses cheese themed magic spells. Here’s a cheese-mancer romancer. A quesomancer, if you will. His spells include The Cheese Touch, and Cheesy Ice Knife (same as ice knife but all knives created are types of cheese knives), and cheese breath (dragon breath). With role playing like this, I scoff when my DM tries to implement stuff like this meme. I’m here for chaos.
Enchantment, you make someone fall in love with the frog, kissing it and reversing the transmutation.

item.title: A photo of my dog Panko every day
item.description: Had day off. Trying to do work on computer. Panko come bug me wants my undying attention. I sit on the floor with him. He leaves ? not even two minutes. Ok. I go back to computer. Rinse repeat all day.

item.title: Great Hammerhead Sharks swim sideways so their big dorsal fins acts like a foil, providing lift and requiring less energy to swim
item.description: This large Great Hammerhead Shark was swimming close to shore by Clearwater, Florida this morning. I've been seeing more Hammerheads in the Clearwater / St Pete area this year than I have for at least a decade. I've seen one every day for the last week or so. All of the pics and videos I post are taken by me. For more OC nature vids check my profile or look for See Through Canoe Company on YouTube, BlueSky, FB & other social media sites.
item.dc:creator: SeeThroughCanoe

item.title: Watching Firefly as a Woman Hits Different
item.description: Sorry to show you a sliver of what I deal with in my day to day life, but I think the full conversation was worthy of having. Also, admittedly, this one was just funny. Also, currently Live Chatting on this video today at 7:30am PST: we’re talking Firefly AGAIN, fake feminism, “strong female characters,” weird subscriber DMs, and why this show hits VERY differently after transitioning. I’ll be live in chat during the premiere ? https://youtu.be/WA6dUAZF-hE
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley
